title: Training CNNs in Practice Capstone
concept: Phase 5 Capstone: We have mastered data augmentation, preprocessing pipelines, class imbalance remedies, debugging strategies, learning rate sweeps, and test-time voting. This completes our practical training foundation.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the latency cost of a 10-pass TTA pipeline relative to a single-pass model?

options.text: It takes the exact same amount of time

exercises.explain: Yes! Latency scales linearly with the number of TTA passes (10x).

cards.title: A Complete Training Arsenal
cards.body: You started this phase with named CNN backbones (Phase 4). But backbones alone are just empty boxes. Over these 8 units, you've built the complete engineering arsenal needed to train, debug, and optimize these backbones in the real world.

cards.title: Phase 5 Recap
cards.body:
We have mastered:
- **Augmentation**: Geometric (rotation/crop) & Photometric (jitter/noise) to expand variety.
- **Blending**: Mixup & CutMix to smooth decision boundaries.
- **Pipelines**: Chaining preprocessing (resize/normalize) in correct order.
- **Imbalance**: Oversampling/weights/metrics.
- **Optimization**: LR Finder/schedulers.
- **Evaluation**: TTA ensembling.

cards.title: Building a custom race car
cards.body: Phase 4 was like buying the engine (ResNet backbone). Phase 5 was tuning the engine: choosing fuel lines (pipelines), balancing suspension (class weights), debugging diagnostic codes (loss curves), adjusting the accelerator (learning rate finder), and adding stability control (TTA). The car is now ready for the track.

cards.title: A Real-world Engineering Project
cards.body: When building an animal classifier for a nature app: you set up a ResNet-50 backbone, balance classes with augmented oversampling, find the LR with an LR Finder, schedule learning with Cosine Annealing, and run TTA in memory on user uploads. This is real-world ML engineering.

cards.title: Tuning data is as important as tuning models
cards.body: It's a myth that ML engineering is only about tweaking layers or building fancy neural code. In practice, tuning your preprocessing, augmentations, learning rate schedules, and data balance has a far larger impact on final accuracy than changing backbone models.

left.text:
Geometric + Photometric Augmentation
Mixup & CutMix regularization
Train vs Val preprocessing splits

right.text:
Weighted loss & augmented balance
LR Finder + Cosine Annealing schedules
TTA soft-voting ensembling

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the correct sequence of steps in a standard CNN training pipeline?

options.text: Load raw batch -> apply training augmentations -> resize & normalize -> forward pass -> calculate loss -> update weights
Normalize -> calculate loss -> apply augmentations -> update weights
Forward pass -> update weights -> apply augmentations -> resize
Delete validation data -> train on random weights only

exercises.explain: Correct! Load, augment, resize & normalize, predict, evaluate, and update.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Tuning preprocessing, data balance, and learning rate schedules usually has a larger impact on final model accuracy than swapping similar CNN backbones.
exercises.explain: True. In real-world projects, data and optimization tuning shape performance far more than swapping similar model architectures.

exercises.sentence: Data augmentations are applied in memory on the fly during training, leaving raw files on disk ___.
exercises.explain: On-the-fly augmentation changes data in RAM, keeping source disk files untouched.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary role of a learning rate scheduler like Cosine Annealing?

options.text: To decay the learning rate smoothly during training, enabling precise convergence late in the run
To double the size of the convolutional kernels every 10 epochs
To automatically label validation images with 100% accuracy

exercises.explain: Correct! Schedulers decay the learning rate smoothly for clean, precise convergence.

exercises.prompt: Phase 5: Training CNNs in Practice is complete. What is the next phase of our Computer Vision curriculum?

options.text: Phase 5: Training CNNs in Practice is complete. Next up: Image Classification in Depth

exercises.explain: Phase 5: Training CNNs in Practice is complete. Next up: Image Classification in Depth. Let's move on to the next phase!
